task_completion.description: Open the Jurism application and view the library
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Jurism application window is open, displaying a list of items that includes Supreme Court cases like 'Brown v. Board of Education' and 'Gideon v. Wainwright'.

task_completion.description: Link 'Brown v. Board of Education' and 'Tinker v. Des Moines Independent Community School District'
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Jurism interface, with either 'Brown v. Board of Education' or 'Tinker v. Des Moines...' selected, the other case is visible in the 'Related' tab in the right-hand pane, or a 'Related' selection dialog shows them being linked.

task_completion.description: Link 'Gideon v. Wainwright' and 'Miranda v. Arizona'
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Jurism interface, with either 'Gideon v. Wainwright' or 'Miranda v. Arizona' selected, the other case is visible in the 'Related' tab in the right-hand pane, or a 'Related' selection dialog shows them being linked.

task_completion.description: Link 'New York Times Co. v. Sullivan' and 'Tinker v. Des Moines Independent Community School District'
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Jurism interface, with either 'New York Times Co. v. Sullivan' or 'Tinker v. Des Moines...' selected, the other case is visible in the 'Related' tab in the right-hand pane, or a 'Related' selection dialog shows them being linked.

integrity.description: The agent used the Jurism GUI to create the links
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent clicking through the Jurism interface (e.g., 'Related' tab, 'Add' button) rather than executing SQLite commands in a terminal to modify the database directly.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a script to bypass the UI
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no evidence of the agent writing or running Python/Bash scripts to interact with the Jurism database.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The database contains 5 Supreme Court cases and 5 law review articles. The full name for the Tinker case in the database is 'Tinker v. Des Moines Independent Community School District'. In Jurism, adding a 'Related' link from Case A to Case B automatically makes it bidirectional, so the agent only needs to add the link once per pair. The VLM should look for the 'Related' tab in the right-hand pane of Jurism or the 'Add Related Items' dialog to verify the links.
